# Nuthatch

> A self-hosted-first, AI-native blockchain indexer in one Rust binary. From a contract
> address to a live indexed API in under two minutes, with no mandatory third-party data
> service and nothing phoning home.

Nuthatch indexes EVM chains on your own hardware. Embedded mode runs as a single static
binary with no external services: no Postgres, no Docker, no IPFS. It works against your own
node (reth ExEx mode) or any RPC. It is free and licensed AGPL-3.0. Status: pre-release.

Tagline: "Be your own indexer."

## What it does

- `nuthatch init 0xAddr --chain mainnet` resolves the ABI (Sourcify, then Etherscan-class
  APIs) and scaffolds an indexer: schema, incremental views, handlers, and a passing test suite.
- `nuthatch dev` follows the chain tip and serves one local endpoint: SQL, GraphQL, and MCP.
- Design targets: under 2 minutes from `init` to first indexed query; at most 2 GB RAM for a
  single chain, tip-following and serving, enforced as a CI budget.

## How it works

- Ingestion (RPC extraction, or reth ExEx when colocated) -> deterministic decode -> entities
  as declarative incremental views (IVM) -> a hot store (redb) for point-reads at the tip ->
  content-addressed Parquet segments sealed past finality -> embedded DuckDB attached read-only
  for analytical SQL.
- Reorgs touch only the mutable hot store and become retractions in the incremental views. The
  columnar layer is append-only by construction.
- The escape hatch is capability-isolated WASM component handlers, in any language via WIT. A
  component granted zero capabilities is a pure function and can be re-executed and verified.

## Principles

- Single static binary; zero external services in embedded mode.
- No phone-home: no telemetry, no mandatory API tokens, no gated data service, ever.
- Deterministic core: decode, reorg handling, and entity derivation are reproducible. LLMs
  generate code and tests; LLM output never sits in the runtime data path.
- AI-native: a built-in MCP server, semantic schema discovery, shipped `llms.txt` and editor
  skills, local models via Ollama or bring-your-own-key. Every AI feature degrades gracefully
  offline.

## Install

- Shell: `curl -fsSL https://nuthatch-indexer.com/install.sh | sh`
- Cargo: `cargo install nuthatch`
- Homebrew: `brew install nuthatch`
- Docker is only needed for scaled mode (Postgres + DataFusion), not embedded mode.

## Worked example

`/example` walks through rebuilding a real community Graph Protocol subgraph
(PaulieB14/horizon-indexer-subgraph, credited to Paul Barba) as a Nuthatch "nest": indexing
Graph Horizon staking on Arbitrum One across three contracts (HorizonStaking, SubgraphService,
StakingExtension). It shows one `init` over three addresses, raw event tables queryable during
backfill, the subgraph's imperative AssemblyScript handlers rebuilt as declarative incremental
views (reorgs retract; no rollback code), golden tests, and serving over SQL, GraphQL, SSE, and
MCP. A "nest" is the unit of indexing definition: a directory of config and views you can
commit, share, and re-run (`nuthatch init --from <git-url>`).

## Links

- Website: https://nuthatch-indexer.com/
- Install guide: https://nuthatch-indexer.com/install
- Worked example (subgraph to nest): https://nuthatch-indexer.com/example

- Source and docs: https://github.com/cargopete/nuthatch
- Full description for models: https://nuthatch-indexer.com/llms-full.txt
- Licence: AGPL-3.0-only
